english: to all great saints and divine beings; it will suddenly break forth from the bodies of the terrifying gods in the shape of fire, which puts the demons to flight Glr.; raṅ-byuṅ ye-śés (rang byung ye shes) the self-originated wisdom occasion-ally is personified in a similar manner, as Wisdom is in the Proverbs of Solomon; in later times this conception coincides in the po-pular mind also with stoṅ-pa-ñyíd (stong pa nyid). — 2. pro-vinc. for yin (yin) Glr. 75. — 3. provinc. for ...am (am), kyed blá-ma-ćan ⸰gró-ye (khyed bla ma can 'gro ye) Mil. are you going to the Lama? — 4. in comp. for ye-śés (ye shes), v. ye-tig (ye thig). — 5. num. figure: 114.
Sch.: 'the trace, line, or mani-festation of divine wisdom'.
Bal. for ñyíd-raṅ (nyid rang), you, the pronoun of polite address.
a contagious disease Cs.; acc. to oral explanation: injury inflicted on the soul, harm done to the mind, which may take place in 360 different ways Mil. —
n. of a city, next to Khobom (Katmandu), the first in Nepal Mil.
v. yér-re-ba (yer re ba).
Jesus Chr. Prot.
rough, shaggy, hairy Cs.
v. yyéṅ-ba (g.yeng ba).
provinc. for yág-po (yag po).

prob. only in yén-la (yen la) joined to ytóṅ-ba (gtong ba) and synonyms, to bestow liber-ally, amply, plentifully; zas daṅ spyód-lam yen-la rtad-par bya (zas dang spyod lam yen la rtad par bya) food and exercise should be amply provided for Lt.
Lt. = yñyíd-yer (gnyid yer) q.v.

phyág-tu yér-pa źig mdzád-nas (phyag tu yer pa zhig mdzad nas) to raise one's hand with the palm turned upward, as a gesture of (wil-lingly or respectfully) offering, Mil.nt. (This term might perh. be applied to the 'waving' of the wave-offerings, ordained by the Mo-saic law.)
sprinkled, sputtered, spouted(?) Sch.
pure, clear, genuine, un-adulterated Mil.; sṅo ye-ré (sngo ye re) a pure blue, dkar ye-ré (dkar ye re) a pure white C.
Pth. frq., e.g. mdaṅs yel-yél (mdangs yel yel), sems-dgá yel-yél (sems dga' yel yel) clear, light, bright or something like it(?).
ancestor Sch.
numerical figure: 144.
Ssk. = rnal-⸰byór (rnal 'byor), yó-gi (yo gi) = yó-ga-pa (yo ga pa), yó-gi-ni (yo gi ni) = yó-ga-ma (yo ga ma); more about this word v. Williams Ssk. Dict.

1. adj. and sbst., oblique, slop-ing, slanting, awry, crooked; obliquity, slope, slant; ćuṅ-yo-ba (cung yo ba) a little slanting, crooked Glr.; ka yo (kha yo) the mouth awry S.g.; yóṅ-po (yong po), col. *yón-te* (*yon te*), adj., id.; yo sróṅ-ba (yo srong ba), yóṅ-po bsraṅ-ba (yong po bsrang ba), Lexx., to make the crooked straight; *zám-pa yon-yóṅ ⸰co dug* (*zam pa yon yong 'co dug*) W. the bridge is unsteady, swings to and fro; fig. twisted, distorted, perverted, erroneous; yon-dpyad (yon dpyad) wrong interpretation, false judgment; going crooked ways, deceitful, crafty, and sbst. crookedness, deceitful dealings Cs.; more frq. yyo (g.yo). — 2. everything, altogether. whole (?) Sch.

tools, implements, chattels, household furniture, necessaries, tsó-bai (tsho ba'i) necessaries of life; mćod-pai (mchod pa'i) requisites for sacrificing; yo-byád sbyór-ba (yo byad sbyor ba) to procure the needful, to make preparations Dzl.; yo-byád tams-ćád-kyis (yo byad thams cad kyis) (or bzaṅ-pos (bzang pos)) stób-pa (stob pa) Tar. to provide a person with everything necessary, to fit out well; yo-byád srél-ba (yo byad srel ba) id. (?) Sch.; yo-byád-kyis ⸰brál-ba (yo byad kyis 'bral ba) to be in want of the needful; nor phyugs yo-byád (nor phyugs yo byad) money, cattle, and furniture, as a specifi-cation of property.
Wdṅ. n. of a tree, which by the Lamas of Sikim is stated to grow in Tibet; Sch.: elm, and in another place: rii yo-⸰bóg (ri'i yo 'bog) linden-tree, less prob.
1. col. but also sometimes in B., for ⸰og ('og) below, down stairs, yog-káṅ (yog khang) ground-floor; cellar. — 2. v. yyóg-pa (g.yog pa).
